kv.memory.bridge-slack-src-bridge-rs-107: bridge-slack/src/bridge.rs:107 byte-slice '&raw_text[..MAX_SLACK_TEXT]' on a &str panics on multi-byte UTF-8 chars at the boundary. Fix: use raw_text.char_indices().take_while(|(i,_)| *i < MAX_SLACK_TEXT).last().map(|(i,c)| &raw_text[..i+c.len_utf8()]).unwrap_or(raw_text) or similar. Confirmed in round 47 review.
kv.memory.decision-chat-is-the-only-mess: DECISION: chat is the only message entry point. nie send subcommand removed. One-shot non-interactive messaging is not a use case.
kv.memory.decision-directory-is-persiste: DECISION: Directory is persistent. The user directory contains every pub_id that has ever successfully authenticated, not just currently-connected users. Online/offline status tracked separately.
kv.memory.decision-key-separation-ed2551: DECISION: Key separation — Ed25519 identity key is signing-only. When MLS is added, a separate X25519/HPKE key must be generated for encryption key exchange. Never convert the Ed25519 key to X25519 for encryption (ed25519_to_x25519 conversion is considered a design smell). The Ed25519 key signs MLS KeyPackages; a fresh ECDH key in the KeyPackage does the actual key agreement.
kv.memory.decision-phase-3-subscription-: DECISION: Phase 3 subscription payments use in-house Zcash Sapling shielded wallet (Option A). BTCPay Server deferred as a future alternative path. Reason: existing nie-wallet already has scanner/address-gen/lightwalletd client; BTCPay lacks Sapling shielded support.
kv.memory.decision-remove-nie-add-and-ni: DECISION: Remove nie add and nie contacts CLI commands. Contact book (contacts.json, config.rs) left intact for future DM phases but CLI subcommands removed — no 1:1 routing in broadcast model.
kv.memory.decision-single-room-architect: DECISION: Single room architecture. nie has exactly one chat room shared by all connected users. No named rooms, no channels, no topics.
kv.memory.decision-wire-protocol-is-json: DECISION: wire protocol is JSON-RPC 2.0 over WebSocket. Client-to-relay requests use {jsonrpc:'2.0',id,method,params}. Server-to-client pushes (deliver, user_joined, etc.) are JSON-RPC notifications (no id). Error responses use the standard JSON-RPC error object {code,message,data}. This replaces the current serde tag-discriminated RelayMessage enum format.
kv.memory.design-db-enum-strings-wallets: DESIGN-db-enum-strings: WalletStore uses explicit match arms (chain_to_str, state_to_str, etc.) to convert enums to SQLite TEXT, not serde rename attributes. This means SQLite values are stable regardless of how serde representations evolve. If a variant is renamed in Rust, the DB string stays the same and the mismatch is caught at compile time by the exhaustive match.
kv.memory.design-from-guard-dispatch-pay: DESIGN-from-guard: dispatch_payment checks from != session.peer_pub_id before processing every incoming PaymentAction. This is the payment-layer analogue of the relay sender-identity check — it prevents a third party who guesses a session_id from injecting forged state transitions into a payment session.
kv.memory.design-sqlite-amount-cast-wall: DESIGN-sqlite-amount-cast: WalletStore uses i64::try_from(u64) for all amount_zatoshi values before binding to SQLite, with a descriptive Err rather than a silent as-cast. This catches unreachable overflow without panicking and leaves a clear audit trail. Pattern established in upsert_session, insert_note, insert_tx.
kv.memory.design-wallet-db-migration-wal: DESIGN-wallet-db-migration: WalletStore uses PRAGMA user_version as a migration version counter. Each migration block is if version < N { ... PRAGMA user_version = N }. Migrations are append-only — never modify a shipped migration, only add a new one after it. v1→v2 tested in v1_data_survives_v2_migration.
kv.memory.profile-fields-set-via-iam-key: /iam <name> sets display name (existing). Profile fields: /set <key> <value> (set), /unset <key> (delete), /profile [handle] (display own or other). Option B chosen for orthogonality — one job per command, no parse tricks.
kv.memory.zcash-primitives-0-26-x-and-zc: sapling-crypto 0.6.2 on crates.io still depends on core2 = 0.3.x (yanked). Use git dep: sapling = { package = "sapling-crypto", git = "https://github.com/zcash/sapling-crypto.git", rev = "b8a81c22f034d68f9bbd6cba728aab807b9ba2ea", default-features = false }. zip32 = 0.2.1 is clean (no core2). In Rust code, the dep alias "sapling" resolves correctly — deps must be placed in [dependencies] section, not after [build-dependencies]. zcash_primitives 0.26.4 on crates.io may still have core2 transitively — not yet tested.
